How Google’s next-gen LiteRT.js and a zero-config Flutter package allow developers to build 100% local, private, offline-capable, and cost-free AI applications.
The AI revolution has accustomed us to a highly centralized architecture. Every time your application interacts with a language model or processes an image, the user’s private data travels to remote servers controlled by tech giants.
This design choice not only raises significant data privacy concerns, but it also introduces a severe financial barrier for independent developers and startups: unsustainable API and cloud infrastructure costs at scale.
What if you could offload that heavy computation directly to your user’s device? Today, with the official release of Google’s LiteRT.js and our new Flutter package saia_lite_rt on pub.dev, local edge AI execution in the browser is no longer a technical pipe dream—it is a production-ready standard.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

1. The Evolutionary Leap: From TensorFlow Lite to LiteRT
For years, TensorFlow Lite has been the go-to runtime engine for mobile on-device machine learning in Android and iOS…
